about.subtitle: InteractJob connects thousands of Moroccan candidates with the best companies in the Kingdom every day. Our mission: simplify recruitment and open employment doors to everyone.
about.missionTitle: Connecting Moroccan talent with the best opportunities
about.missionDesc1: Created by recruitment professionals passionate about the Moroccan job market, InteractJob was born from a simple conviction: every candidate deserves easy access to the best job offers, and every company deserves to quickly find the talent they seek.
about.missionDesc2: We aggregate offers from the best Moroccan job boards (Rekrute.com, Emploi.ma, Bayt.com) and add direct offers from partner companies — all in a modern, fast and intuitive interface.
about.v1Desc: Making the Moroccan job market transparent, accessible and fair for all candidates.
about.v2Desc: Each offer is verified before publication. We guarantee legitimate and quality listings.
about.v3Desc: We use the best digital tools to effectively connect talent and recruiters.
about.v4Desc: InteractJob is 100% dedicated to the Moroccan market, with deep knowledge of its economic fabric.
about.linkedinDesc: Join more than 18,000 Moroccan professionals who follow our HR news, career tips and exclusive job offers.
about.contactDesc: A question, partnership or offer to post? Our team is available.

footer.desc: The job platform dedicated to the Moroccan market. Join over 18,000 professionals who trust us every day.

donation.modalBody: InteractJob is 100% free with no intrusive ads. If the site helped you find a job or improve your CV, a small coffee helps us keep going!

concours.title: Public Sector Recruitment Competitions in Morocco
concours.activeSummary: {count, plural, =0 {active competitions} one {# active competition} other {# active competitions}}
concours.prepareDesc: Maximize your chances of success. An optimized CV, a compelling cover letter and a complete file make all the difference at the pre-selection stage.
concours.privateSectorTitle: After the competition, find a job in the private sector
concours.privateSectorDesc: Don't bet everything on one competition — the private sector is hiring too.
concours.introTitle: How public-sector recruitment competitions work in Morocco
concours.introP1: A Moroccan public-sector competition (concours) is an official recruitment procedure run by a ministry, a local authority, or a public institution, under the general civil service statute. Each competition is announced in an official notice specifying the number of positions, the required diploma level, the tests involved, and the application deadline.
concours.introP2: The process generally has three stages: file-based pre-selection (checking diplomas and eligibility), written tests (general knowledge, a subject-specific test, sometimes languages), then an oral interview for shortlisted candidates. The time between a competition opening and final results being published ranges from 2 to 6 months depending on the institution.
concours.introP3: To apply, prepare a complete file: an up-to-date CV, a copy of your national ID card, certified copies of your diplomas, a cover letter, and any document specified in the notice (medical certificate, criminal record extract, etc.). An incomplete file or one submitted after the deadline is automatically rejected — always check the conditions on the institution's official website before applying.
concours.introP4a: InteractJob tracks every public-sector competition in Morocco daily from official sources. See our
concours.introP4b: for detailed advice, and use our free tools to optimize your CV before submitting your application.
concours.faqQ1: How do I apply for a public competition in Morocco?
concours.faqA1: Check the official notice published by the hiring institution (ministry, local authority, or public institution), verify the eligibility requirements (diploma, age, nationality), then submit your file before the stated deadline — either online via the relevant administration's portal, or by registered mail as specified in the announcement.
concours.faqQ2: What documents do I need for a public-sector competition?
concours.faqA2: Generally: an up-to-date CV, a copy of your national ID card (CIN), certified copies of your diplomas and certificates, a cover letter, and sometimes a criminal record extract or a medical certificate. The exact list is always specified in the official competition notice.
concours.faqQ3: Can I apply for several competitions at the same time?
concours.faqA3: Yes, there is no legal limit on the number of public competitions a candidate can apply to simultaneously, as long as the eligibility requirements and deadlines of each competition are respected. Many candidates apply to several to increase their chances.
concours.faqQ4: How long does the recruitment process for a public competition take?
concours.faqA4: The timeline varies by institution, but generally takes between 2 and 6 months between the application deadline and the publication of final results, including file pre-selection, written tests, and the oral interview.
concours.sourcesText: Sources: Moroccan public employment platforms. Always verify the official announcement on the relevant institution's portal.
concours.searchPlaceholder: Search a competition by ministry or city…
concours.sourceAttribution: Found via {source} — always verify the original announcement on the institution's website before applying.
concours.descIntro: {org} has opened a recruitment competition in Morocco. This type of notice falls under the official recruitment procedures of the Moroccan civil service, guaranteeing transparency and equal opportunity for all candidates.
concours.descNiveau: This competition is open to candidates holding a {niveau} level.
concours.descPostes: {count, plural, one {This recruitment concerns # position to be filled.} other {This recruitment concerns # positions to be filled.}} Successful candidates will join the civil service with a salary, benefits, and professional stability in line with the general civil service statute.
concours.descPrepare: To put together a complete application file, you will generally need: an up-to-date CV, a copy of your national ID card, certified copies of your diplomas, a cover letter, and any other document specified in the official notice. Be sure to check the eligibility conditions on {org}'s official website before submitting your application.
concours.descOutro: InteractJob tracks every public-sector, local-authority and public-institution recruitment competition in Morocco. Use our free tools to optimize your CV and maximize your chances of success. An ATS-optimized CV can make all the difference at the pre-selection stage, even for public competitions.
